body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` **Triggered by:** commit 685f2f019a1387dac852b731ef7b9e4632eeacb0 (PR #58396) by @RaviPidaparthi ## What Changed PR #58396 introduced the following public API changes to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (targeting `1.0.0-beta.23`): - **Added `PlatformHeaders` static class** — centralizes all platform HTTP header name constants (`x-request-id`, `x-platform-server`, `x-agent-session-id`, isolation keys, `traceparent`, `x-ms-client-request-id`). Replaces per-package `private const` declarations. - **Added `RequestIdMiddleware`** — sets the `x-request-id` response header on every HTTP response (OTEL trace ID → incoming header → GUID fallback). Registered automatically by `AgentHostBuilder` and by `AddAgentServerCore()` for Tier 3. - **Added `RequestIdBaggagePropagator`** — propagates `x-request-id` into `Activity.Baggage` for distributed trace correlation. - **Breaking change**: Removed `IsolationContext.UserIsolationKeyHeaderName` and `IsolationContext.ChatIsolationKeyHeaderName` — replaced by `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` and `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey`. - **Breaking change**: Replaced 6 individual `Add/Use` extension methods with a single `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` pair for Tier 3 setups. The README had only a trivial one-line change (CHANGELOG link update) and does not reflect these new APIs. ## Gaps Found - `PlatformHeaders` is a new public type with no entry in the **Key concepts** section of the README — users building Tier 3 setups or referencing header names need to know it exists. - The `x-request-id` response header behavior (added automatically, priority order, error correlation via `additionalInfo.request_id`) is not mentioned anywhere in the README. - The Troubleshooting / Logging section mentions `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` but does not explain the breaking change from the previous six individual extension methods, leaving upgraders without context. - No mention of `RequestIdBaggagePropagator` and how it enables end-to-end distributed trace correlation for Tier 3 standalone setups.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` — **edit** 1. **Add a `PlatformHeaders` entry to the Key concepts section** (after the existing `FoundryEnvironment` entry): ```markdown ### PlatformHeaders A static class that centralizes all platform HTTP header name constants used across AgentServer packages: `x-request-id`, `x-platform-server`, `x-agent-session-id`, `x-user-isolation-key`, `x-chat-isolation-key`, `traceparent`, and `x-ms-client-request-id`. Use `PlatformHeaders` constants in custom middleware or isolation-aware logic instead of declaring private string constants. > **Upgrading from beta.22?** `IsolationContext.UserIsolationKeyHeaderName` and > `IsolationContext.ChatIsolationKeyHeaderName` have been removed — use > `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` and `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` instead. ``` 2. **Add a `Request ID propagation` entry to the Key concepts section** (after `PlatformHeaders`): ```markdown ### Request ID propagation Every HTTP response includes an `x-request-id` header set by `RequestIdMiddleware`. The value is resolved in priority order: OTEL trace ID → incoming `x-request-id` request header → newly generated GUID. This allows clients to correlate a failed response with backend telemetry. For Tier 1 and Tier 2 setups the middleware is registered automatically; for Tier 3 it is included in `AddAgentServerCore()`. Error response bodies are enriched with `error.additionalInfo.request_id` matching the header value, enabling client-side error correlation without parsing response headers separately. `RequestIdBaggagePropagator` additionally injects the incoming `x-request-id` value into `Activity.Baggage`, making it available to downstream spans in distributed traces. ``` 3. **Update the Troubleshooting → Logging paragraph** to mention the migration from the old six-method API: Replace the current sentence: > "for Tier 3, call `AddAgentServerCore()` and `UseAgentServerCore()` to enable it." With: > "For Tier 3, call `AddAgentServerCore()` and `UseAgentServerCore()` to enable request ID propagation, > version headers, and inbound request logging in a single pair of calls. (Prior to `1.0.0-beta.23`, > these were six separate `Add/Use` extension methods; those methods have been removed.)" ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet test files are required for this change — the concepts are architectural/configuration explanations that don't need test-backed code samples. The existing `Core_ReadMe_CreateBuilder` snippet already demonstrates the builder pattern. If a snippet showing `PlatformHeaders` usage would be helpful, add it in a new sample method in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/tests/Samples/` following the pattern in `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required sections in correct order: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Start a server) 2. ⚠️ Key concepts — missing `PlatformHeaders` and Request ID propagation entries 3. ✅ Examples 4. ⚠️ Troubleshooting — Logging paragraph needs updating 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public API changes expected from this docs-only edit 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [AgentServer] Docs: Document PlatformHeaders, RequestIdMiddleware, and x-request-id in Core README
